text.title: Compound Component Pattern
text.summary: Compound components share state through React context while exposing a readable sub-component API. Export sub-components as named exports as well as via Object.assign to preserve tree-shaking.

rule.statement: Create a context for shared state, a Root component that provides it, and sub-components that consume it. Export sub-components as both named exports and as Object.assign properties.
rule.rationale: Object.assign-only export prevents tree-shaking; named exports let bundlers drop unused sub-components.

rule.statement: Throw a descriptive error from useXContext() when used outside the Provider, not a silent null.
rule.rationale: Silent nulls from missing context produce cryptic rendering failures; a thrown error with the component name lets the agent diagnose instantly.

rule.statement: Use data-state="open|closed" attributes instead of conditional class strings for state-driven styling.
rule.rationale: Radix-style data attributes simplify both CSS selectors and test assertions; conditional class strings scatter state logic across the component.
